title: 10 best herbal capsules for oily hair

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame capsules helps with oily hair because they contain sesamin, a potent natural compound that has been shown to reduce oil production in the scalp. This helps to combat excessive sebum secretion, leaving hair looking healthier and more manageable. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an itchy or flaky scalp often associated with oily hair. As a result, herbal sesame capsules provide a natural solution for achieving a balanced and healthy-looking mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Sesame capsules contain antioxidants that help combat free radicals, which can contribute to oily scalp and hair problems by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sesame capsules can help reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, which can be triggered by oily hair, and promote healthy blood flow to the hair follicles.

Sebum-regulating property: Sesame oil has been traditionally used to regulate sebum production, helping to balance the natural oils of the scalp and hair, thereby reducing excessive oiliness and promoting a healthier, more manageable hair texture.
remedy_constituents: Sesamin: Sesamin is a lignan present in Sesamum indicum, which has been shown to have a potential role in reducing oil production in the scalp, thereby helping to manage oily hair.

Sesamolin: Sesamolin is another lignan found in Sesamum indicum, which has been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help to regulate oil production and prevent clogged pores, contributing to a reduction in oily hair.

Fatty Acids (especially Oleic and Linoleic Acid): Fatty acids present in Sesamum indicum may help to regulate the scalp's natural oil production, balance the pH of the scalp, and prevent excessive oil secretion, which can contribute to oily hair.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Sesamum indicum are commonly used due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help control oil production and reduce scalp irritation.
Leaves: Sesamum indicum leaves are used because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and balance the scalp, reducing oiliness.
(Optional) Seeds: Another part of Sesamum indicum is seeds, which is also used due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help control oil production and reduce scalp irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the sesamum indicum seeds in a dry and cool environment with minimal light exposure.
Clean and dry the sesamum indicum seeds thoroughly to remove any debris or contaminants immediately.
Grind the dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder within 1 hour.
Mix the sesamum indicum powder with a binding agent in a ratio of 1:3 and blend well for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the sesamum indicum mixture and seal them tightly within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with oily hair because they contain natural ingredients that balance the scalp's oil production. The aloe vera gel in the capsules helps to regulate sebum levels, reducing excess oil and product buildup. This leaves hair feeling cleaner and lighter, without stripping it of its natural moisture. As a result, users may experience improved manageability, reduced greasiness, and a more balanced scalp environment, making it an effective supplement for those with oily hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness and irritation associated with oily scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Antimicrobial property: Herbal aloe capsules' antimicrobial property helps eliminate bacteria, fungi, and yeast that contribute to oily buildup on the scalp, preventing clogged pores and reducing dandruff.

Moisturizing property: Aloe vera's moisturizing property helps balance the scalp's natural moisture levels, reducing oil production and preventing dryness, itchiness, and flakiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in Aloe vera, such as ferulic acid and chlorogenic acid, help with oily hair by inhibiting the production of sebum, thereby reducing oiliness and promoting a balanced scalp.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, particularly aloin and aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and conditions like dandruff.

Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones, such as aloe-emodin, have astringent properties that help to tighten the scalp's pores, reducing oil secretion and preventing excessive oiliness, while also promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Gel from Leaves: This part is widely used due to its soothing and moisturizing properties that help balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production.
Extract from Leaves: This part is commonly used because it contains salicylic acid, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control scalp infections and reduce oiliness.
Juice from Leaves: This part is often used due to its ability to help regulate the scalp's natural oil production, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature plants weighing at least 1-2 kg each.
Extract the clear gel from aloe vera leaves using a spoon or scoop.
Mix the extracted gel with a small amount of distilled water approximately 10-20 grams.
Fill gelatin capsules with the aloe vera gel mixture approximately 500-750 mg each.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with oily hair because they stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy circulation and reducing oil production. The antioxidants and essential oils present in rosemary also help to cleanse and purify the hair follicles, removing impurities and toxins that can contribute to excess oiliness. As a result, rosemary capsules can help to balance the scalp's natural oil production, leaving your hair feeling cleaner, lighter, and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can damage hair follicles, leading to excessive oil production and an oily scalp.
Stimulant Properties: Rosemary's stimulant properties increase blood flow to the scalp, which helps to remove impurities and toxins that can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.

Antifungal and Antibacterial Properties: Rosemary's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can cause flaking, itchiness, and oil production.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Helps reduce oil production by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone associated with hair oiliness.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help regulate the scalp's natural oil balance, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy hair growth.
Bornyl acetate: Has astringent properties that help tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing oil secretion and preventing the buildup of excess oil, which can lead to oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils that help regulate scalp oil production.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their antiseptic and astringent properties, which help to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce oiliness.
Stems: The stems are used for their ability to absorb and extract the beneficial compounds from the leaves and flowers, making them a convenient and concentrated ingredient.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from the plant 2-3 months after the flowering period.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 10g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered rosmarinus officinalis with 5g of gelatin or vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetable cellulose capsules with 250mg of the rosmarinus officinalis blend and seal securely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with oily hair because they reduce inflammation and regulate oil production in the scalp. The capsules contain compounds that help to normalize sebum levels, leaving your hair looking healthy and balanced. By reducing excess oil production, stinging nettle capsules can help to prevent greasiness and itchiness, making them an effective natural remedy for oily hair. Additionally, their anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated scalps and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Nettle capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage the hair follicles and lead to oil production, resulting in oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in nettle capsules can reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is often associated with conditions like dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis that can contribute to oily hair.
Antihormonal properties: Nettle capsules have been shown to regulate hormone imbalances, which can affect oil production in the scalp and lead to oily hair.
remedy_constituents: Steroids (specifically Ursolic acid): Ursolic acid in Urtica dioica may help reduce sebum production and regulate the skin's natural oil balance, potentially contributing to a decrease in oily hair.

Alkaloids (specifically Aucubin): Aucubin, a type of alkaloid found in Urtica dioica, may possess antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and regulate the skin's natural oil production, which could help alleviate oily hair.
Terpenes (specifically Beta-Sitosterol): Beta-Sitosterol, a type of terpene found in Urtica dioica, is known for its ability to block the production of sebum, which may help reduce oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that help to nourish and protect the scalp and hair.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe an itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
Stems: Employed for their ability to provide a natural source of silica, which can help to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair texture.
remedy_recipe: Dry the plant material in a single layer at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1 ounce of dried urtica dioica material into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a carrier agent such as cornstarch.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree capsules helps with oily hair because they contain potent antioxidants that penetrate the scalp, reducing excess oil production. The antiseptic properties of tea tree oil also help to control fungal and bacterial growth, which can contribute to an oily scalp. Additionally, the capsules' natural astringent properties help to balance the scalp's pH levels, reducing the appearance of greasiness and leaving hair feeling clean and refreshed. Overall, tea tree capsules provide a holistic approach to managing oily hair, promoting a healthier and more balanced scalp.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil: Has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to reduce scalp irritation, inflammation, and fungal infections that can contribute to oily hair.
Melaleuca Alternifolia: Contains compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oil production and dandruff.
Cineole: Has antimicrobial properties that help to kill bacteria and fungi that can cause excessive oil secretion in the scalp, promoting a healthier hair follicle environment.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to control oily hair by reducing sebum production and balancing the scalp's natural oil levels.
Terpinen-4-ol: As an antiseptic and antibacterial agent, terpinen-4-ol helps to combat scalp infections and reduce inflammation, leading to a decrease in oily hair and dandruff.
Cymene: This monoterpene has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control the growth of yeast and bacteria on the scalp that can contribute to oily hair and scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Melaleuca alternifolia leaves are used due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which is known for its antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to control oil production and soothe scalp irritations.
Barks: Melaleuca alternifolia barks are used because they contain compounds that can help to balance sebum production, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy scalp environment.
Seeds: Melaleuca alternifolia seeds are used due to their ability to provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits, which can help to calm and soothe an oily scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves from mature plants with minimal exposure to pesticides.
Dry the leaves in a clean environment at 50°c for 3 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 250g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powder with 10% gelatin and 5% glycerin in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the mixture and seal immediately using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsules helps with oily hair because they contain Artemisia absinthium, a natural antifungal and antibacterial agent that targets the root cause of excessive oil production. The capsules work to balance the scalp's natural pH levels, reducing inflammation and slowing down sebum production. This results in reduced oiliness and a healthier scalp, leaving hair looking cleaner, shinier, and more manageable. With regular use, wormwood capsules can help restore balance to oily hair and promote a healthy, balanced scalp.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Wormwood's astringent compounds help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and absorption, which can help alleviate excessive oiliness.

Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial activity helps to combat scalp infections and fungal imbalances that can contribute to oily hair by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that feed on excess sebum.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with oily hair, while also soothing the scalp and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: A terpene that helps reduce sebum production, thereby controlling oily hair.
Artabsin: A sesquiterpene that has been shown to have antiseborrheic properties, which may help alleviate oily scalp conditions.
Artemisinin: A sesquiterpene lactone with potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help soothe and balance the scalp, reducing oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Artemisia absinthium leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat scalp conditions and promote healthy hair growth.
Seeds: Artemisia absinthium seeds contain compounds with antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help control scalp infections and oily hair conditions.
Stems: Artemisia absinthium stems have been used in herbal remedies to help balance the scalp's natural oil production, reducing oily hair and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Dry the absinthium herb by spreading it out in a single layer on a paper bag for 2 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind 4-6 grams of the dried absinthium herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 2 teaspoons of gelatin in a small bowl to create a gel-like mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a gelatin capsule filler and seal the capsules.
Store the absinthium capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with oily hair because they contain antioxidants that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The herbal extract also has astringent properties, which can help to dry out excess sebum and reduce shine. Additionally, sage has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and calm irritation caused by oily hair. By taking sage capsules regularly, individuals with oily hair may find their locks looking healthier, more manageable, and less prone to oiliness.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage's antibacterial compounds, such as camphor and terpenes, help eliminate bacteria on the scalp that can contribute to oil production and clogged pores.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce redness and irritation in the scalp, which can help alleviate flaking and itchiness associated with oily hair.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to excessive oil production, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This phenolic compound helps reduce sebum production in the scalp, thereby controlling oily hair.

Salvianolic acid: This water-soluble phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help regulate oil secretion in the scalp and reduce inflammation, contributing to a healthier scalp and reduced oiliness.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has been shown to inhibit the production of sebum, a key factor in oily hair, by reducing the activity of enzymes involved in sebum synthesis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves contain essential oils that help to balance and regulate oil production in the scalp.
Leaves: The antioxidant properties in the leaves help to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp.
Leaves: The antiseptic properties in the leaves help to prevent bacterial and fungal growth that can contribute to oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Grind 10 grams of the harvested material into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Sift 5 grams of the powder through a 60-mesh sieve to remove lumps and debris.
Mix 0.5 gram of gelatin with 10 milliliters of distilled water in a small saucepan.
Fill 100-count gelatin capsules with 0.1 gram of the sifted salvia officinalis powder each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with oily hair because they contain calendula, a natural herb that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to soothe and calm an irritated scalp, reducing inflammation that can contribute to excess oil production. Additionally, calendula's antioxidant properties help to balance the scalp's pH levels, regulating sebum production and leaving hair looking healthy and manageable. This natural approach helps to reduce frizz, flyaways, and dandruff, leaving you with a balanced and beautiful mane.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold capsules contain flavonoids and carotenoids that reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness by causing irritation and redness.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold capsules help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to an imbalance in sebum production and oily hair.
Astringent properties: Pot marigold capsules contain tannins that help reduce excess oil secretion by tightening the pores of the scalp and balancing the natural moisture levels.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have astringent and antiseptic properties that help reduce oil production and prevent bacterial growth on the scalp, leading to a decrease in oily hair.

Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and balance the scalp, reducing oil production and inflammation associated with oily hair.

Triterpenoids (such as calendulene): These compounds have been shown to have astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce oil production, prevent bacterial growth, and promote a healthy scalp environment, ultimately reducing oily hair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are often used in hair care products due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to balance the scalp and reduce oil production.
Leaves: Leaves of Calendula are also used in hair care products for their astringent properties, which can help to control oil secretion and soothe the scalp.
Seeds: Calendula seeds are sometimes used in hair care products for their emollient properties, which can help to moisturize and balance the scalp, reducing oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a dry sunny morning after 3 hours of dew removal.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the dried flower powder with 1 part of vegetable cellulose in a ratio for capsule filling.
Fill 100 vegetable capsules with the mixture and seal each capsule using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with oily hair because they contain a unique blend of antioxidants and essential fatty acids that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The capsules' potent ingredients work to reduce sebum secretion, leaving the hair feeling lighter and less greasy. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in maidenhair tree help to soothe an irritated scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_properties: Adiantum majus Extract: Contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to balance oil production in the scalp, reducing excess sebum and promoting healthy hair growth.
Vitamin B Complex: Provides essential nutrients for healthy hair follicles, helping to regulate sebum production and strengthen hair roots, leading to less oily and more manageable locks.
Astringent Tannins: Help to absorb excess moisture and reduce oiliness by tightening the pores on the scalp, making it ideal for controlling oily hair.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, in Ginkgo biloba may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to oily hair and scalp conditions.
Terpenoids (Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba may help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation in the scalp, potentially alleviating oily hair and scalp conditions.

Phenolic Acids (Ferulic Acid): Phenolic acids in Ginkgo biloba, such as ferulic acid, may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the scalp from damage and reduce the appearance of oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help balance the scalp's oil production and reduce dandruff.
Seeds: They contain bilobalide, a compound that helps regulate sebum production and soothe an oily scalp.
Barks: They contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Extract ginkgo biloba leaves from dried plants using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin at a 1:1 ratio.
Mix the extracted liquid with a carrier like cornstarch or cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder and set the machine to 0.5 to 1 gram per capsule.
Seal the capsules using a manual or automatic capsule sealer and store them in a cool dark place.
Label the capsules with the ingredients and dosage instructions which should be taken 2 to 3 capsules daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with oily hair because they contain potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing excessive oil production. The herbs in these capsules also possess astringent qualities, which absorb excess moisture and purify the pores, leaving your locks feeling cleaner and lighter. Additionally, the antioxidants present in purple coneflower capsules help to strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and a reduced appearance of greasiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as artemesinin and luteolin, can help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which may contribute to oily hair by soothing irritation and promoting a healthy balance of sebum production.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The capsules' antifungal and antibacterial agents, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, can help combat fungal and bacterial overgrowth on the scalp, which can lead to an imbalance in the natural oil-producing processes, resulting in oily hair.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's high antioxidant content, comprising vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, helps neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles, leading to scalp irritation, itchiness, and excess oil production.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Echinacea purpurea, such as echinatin and echinacoside, help reduce sebum production in the scalp, thereby reducing oily hair.

Phenolic acids (Caffeic acid): Caffeic acid in Echinacea purpurea has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help regulate scalp oil production and reduce dandruff associated with oily hair.

Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin in Echinacea purpurea has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe scalp irritation and reduce inflammation, leading to a decrease in oil production and oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high content of inulin, which can help regulate the scalp's natural oil production.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce oiliness and prevent scalp irritation.
Leaves: Used due to their content of caffeic acid and other polyphenols, which may help control sebum production and improve scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently washing it with water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned plant material in a temperature-controlled environment at 40°c for 2-3 weeks or until it reaches a moisture content of 10%.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind the dried plant material into a fine powder.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500-1000 mg of the powdered echinacea purpurea and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with oily hair because they promote a healthy scalp by reducing inflammation and soothing irritation. The calming properties of chamomile also help to regulate sebum production, balancing the natural oils in the hair and preventing overproduction. As a result, hair appears healthier and less greasy, while also being more manageable and easier to style. Additionally, chamomile's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to control dandruff and other scalp issues that can contribute to oily hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile capsules can reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can help to calm and soothe irritated skin caused by oily hair, leading to a reduction in flaking and redness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chamomile capsules help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff, promoting healthier and balanced hair growth.

Astringent properties: Chamomile's astringent properties help to tighten and constrict the pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and absorption, resulting in a more balanced and manageable amount of oil in the hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing oil production and soothing scalp irritations.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a natural antiseptic and antibacterial agent, helping to control fungal and bacterial growth on the scalp and regulate sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a reputable supplier or grow your own.
Measure out 50 grams of dried flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered flowers with 100 grams of pure gelatin in a bowl to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty #00 capsules with 200 milligrams of the blended mixture and seal each capsule securely.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months or use immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules help with oily hair because they have natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been used for centuries to balance the skin and scalp, reducing excess oil production and controlling sebum secretions. By taking St John's Wort capsules regularly, individuals can experience a reduction in greasiness and dandruff, leaving their hair looking healthier and more manageable. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of the herb help to soothe and calm the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a potent antioxidant that helps to reduce sebum production by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase, which converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT). This reduction in sebum production can help to alleviate oily hair.

Flavonoids: St John's Wort contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated scalps and reduce redness and flakiness associated with oily hair conditions such as dandruff or psoriasis.

Hypolipidemic activity: St John's Wort has been shown to possess hypolipidemic activity, meaning it helps to lower cholesterol levels in the blood. This can help to reduce the amount of sebum produced by the scalp, leading to healthier, less oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots help to regulate oil production and reduce inflammation in the scalp.
Leaves: Containing salicylic acid and other beta-hydroxy acids, leaves help to exfoliate and unclog pores, reducing excess oil and acne.
Flowers: High in anthraquinones, flowers possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm irritated scalps prone to oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh st john's wort flowers at peak blooming stage for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of ground st john's wort with 10g of silica gel capsules and store in an airtight container.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks to allow the herbs to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with oily hair because they contain citral, a natural compound that has antifungal and antibacterial properties. When consumed as supplements, lemongrass capsules help to regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing excessive oil secretion and controlling dandruff and itchiness. This leads to a reduction in greasiness and a healthy, balanced hair growth. Additionally, lemongrass has natural astringent properties that help to tighten pores, further minimizing oil buildup and leaving hair looking clean and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Lemongrass capsules contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, reducing oil production and preventing clogged pores.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Lemongrass contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and calm an itchy or irritated scalp, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily hair.

Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of lemongrass capsules can help tighten the hair follicles and pores, reducing excess oil secretion and controlling sebum production, leading to healthier-looking hair.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good for making medicinal capsules because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to regulate sebum production and reduce dandruff.
Roots: Roots are used due to their ability to absorb excess oil from the scalp, reducing greasiness and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from your garden or local market and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned leaves in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 12 hours or until crispy, then grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 20g of the ground cymbopogon citratus powder with 80g of microcrystalline cellulose and 10g of silicon dioxide in a clean bowl.
Fill empty capsules shells with the prepared blend and seal according to manufacturer's instructions, yielding 100 capsules per batch.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 1 year, using one capsule twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allspice capsules helps with oily hair because they contain berberine, a natural compound that reduces sebum production in the scalp. Berberine works by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme called 5-alpha-reductase, which is responsible for converting testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to oiliness and dandruff. By reducing DHT levels, berberine helps to regulate sebum production, leaving hair looking healthier, cleaner, and less greasy. Regular use of allspice capsules can lead to a noticeable reduction in oily hair and scalp issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Allspice contains antioxidants like flavonoids, which help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oil production and clogged pores on the scalp.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in allspice, such as tannins, help constrict blood vessels and reduce sebum secretion, leading to a decrease in oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Allspice's anti-inflammatory compounds, like eugenol, can soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation that can contribute to oil production and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the allspice plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce sebum production and balance the scalp.
Buds: Allspice buds contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to purify the scalp and regulate oil secretion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pimenta dioica leaves measuring 100g for subsequent drying.
Dry the pimenta dioica leaves in a warm dry place for 3 days taking regular turns.
Grind the dried pimenta dioica leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground pimenta dioica with 200ml of distilled water and let it steep for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the herbal extract for dosing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules help with oily hair because they contain compounds that stimulate the scalp and improve circulation, reducing excess oil production. The antioxidants present in fennel also help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles, leading to healthier and more balanced locks. Additionally, fennel's astringent properties help to tighten pores on the scalp, reducing sebum buildup and leaving hair feeling fresh and clean. As a result, regular use of fennel capsules can lead to a significant reduction in oiliness and an improvement in overall hair health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to oil production and dandruff.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness, which can occur when hair is excessively oily.

Detoxifying properties: Fennel's natural diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties may help remove impurities and excess sebum from the scalp, reducing oil production and promoting a healthy hair growth environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and antioxidants, which help to absorb excess oil and reduce inflammation in the scalp.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, particularly omega-3 and omega-6, which balance sebum production and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 50 grams of foeniculum vulgare seeds.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered seeds with 100 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of water.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 30 minutes and then heat it slowly until the gelatin dissolves.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the resulting mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime capsules helps with oily hair because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to regulate oil production in the scalp. The capsules also possess antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oily hair. Additionally, the herbal extract has been shown to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, allowing for a healthier and more balanced environment that promotes healthy hair growth and reduces oiliness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Broad-leaved lime capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and lead to an overproduction of sebum, contributing to oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and irritation caused by dandruff, psoriasis, or other scalp conditions that can contribute to oily hair, while also soothing the scalp.
Antibacterial properties: Broad-leaved lime capsules contain compounds that have antibacterial effects, which can help combat fungal infections like malassezia, a common cause of oily scalp and hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce sebum production and soothe scalp irritations.
Barks: Contains tannins and flavonoids that aid in reducing excess oil secretion and tightening pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia platyphyllos flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or air-drying for 7 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and store them in an airtight container.
Mix 1000mg of ground tilia platyphyllos with 500mg of silica gel and 200mg of vegetable glycerin, then fill into capsules.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with oily hair because they contain the natural antifungal and antibacterial properties of lavender, which help to balance the scalp's pH levels and reduce sebum production. This can lead to a reduction in oiliness and an overall healthier hair environment. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can soothe an itchy or flaky scalp, promoting a healthy and manageable mane. As a result, English lavender capsules can be a natural and effective solution for those struggling with oily hair.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Lavender's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, reducing oil production and preventing clogged pores.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flakiness associated with oily hair.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of greasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender are used to create a calming and soothing agent that helps regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness in the hair.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain essential fatty acids that help balance the scalp's natural moisture levels, controlling oil production and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Grind 50g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground flower powder with 250ml of distilled water to create an infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with 250mg each of the infused liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Prairie toothpick capsules helps with oily hair because they contain a blend of natural herbs that work to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The capsules help to absorb excess oil, reducing the appearance of greasiness and leaving hair feeling clean and refreshed. Additionally, the herbal ingredients in the capsules help to purify the scalp, removing impurities and toxins that can contribute to oily hair. As a result, prairie toothpick capsules provide a natural and effective solution for managing oily locks.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Anti-inflammatory Properties: These properties help to reduce bacterial growth on the scalp, which can contribute to oil production and irritation, while also soothing and calming irritated skin.
Astringent Properties: The astringent compounds in Herbal Prairie Toothpick Capsules help to tighten pores, reducing excess oil secretion and leaving hair feeling cleaner and less greasy.
Antioxidant Properties: Antioxidants in the capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles, leading to increased sebum production and oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to balance oil production and reduce sebum secretion.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the scalp and reduce redness associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zanthoxylum americanum bark from sustainable sources ensuring 50-70 grams of dried material for the batch.
Dry the harvested bark using air circulation and temperature control methods taking approximately 7 days to reach optimal dryness.
Grind the dried zanthoxylum americanum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder achieving a uniform consistency.
Mix the powdered zanthoxylum americanum with 150-200 grams of natural silica gel and 50-70 grams of tapioca starch for proper filling and encapsulation.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months ensuring potency and freshness.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for oily hair are natural supplements that help to balance the scalp's oil production, reducing greasiness and preventing dandruff, itchiness, and flakiness. The best herbal capsules for oily hair include Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, Ginkgo biloba, Saponaria officinalis, Aloe vera, Azadirachta indica, and Serenoa repens. If left untreated, oily hair can lead to low self-esteem, social isolation, and emotional distress due to the constant struggle with hair care and styling.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal capsules are conditions characterized by excessive sebum production or imbalance in the body's natural oil regulation. These include acne, hyperhidrosis (excessive sweating), and rosacea. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like saw palmetto, guggul, and tea tree oil may help regulate oil production, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy skin and scalp function.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with oily hair is a blend of Saw Palmetto, Rosemary, and Nettle Root. Saw Palmetto helps regulate oil production and promote a healthy scalp. Rosemary stimulates blood flow and promotes hair growth, while also reducing dandruff and itchiness. Nettle Root acts as a natural detoxifier, removing impurities and excess oil from the scalp. Taking these herbal capsules regularly can help achieve a balanced and healthy scalp, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy-looking hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia capsules helps with oily hair because they contain tea tree oil, a natural antifungal and antibacterial agent that soothes and balances the scalp. The oil's astringent properties help regulate sebum production, reducing excessive oiliness and flakiness. Additionally, tea tree oil's ability to penetrate deep into the hair follicles helps to eliminate bacteria and other impurities that can clog pores and exacerbate oily hair conditions, promoting a healthier and more balanced scalp and hair.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This phenolic compound helps reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to oily scalp conditions.
Linalool: As an antifungal agent, linalool prevents the overgrowth of yeast and other microorganisms that cause oily hair and scalp.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce irritation and inflammation associated with oily hair and scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to control oil production and prevent scalp infections.
Stems: Contain bioflavonoids, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Leaves (again, as one of the primary parts used for its medicinal properties): The leaves also contain terpenes, which help to balance oil production and reduce dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried leaves from mature melaleuca alternifolia plants.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 0.5 grams of the powder with 2 grams of vegetable cellulose in a small bowl.
Fill 10-15 vegetarian capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis capsules helps with oily hair because they contain rosmarinic acid, a natural compound that helps balance the scalp's oil production. This herb has been traditionally used to promote a healthy scalp and reduce dandruff. The antioxidant properties of rosmarinic acid also help to purify the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting a balanced oil flow. As a result, the scalp is able to regulate its natural oil production, leading to less oily hair and a healthier scalp overall. Regular use can lead to a more manageable and balanced hair.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This antioxidant helps to reduce the production of sebum, the oil produced by the hair follicles, thereby controlling oily hair.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the inflammation of the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair.
Cineole: This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and scalp irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which help balance scalp oil production and reduce dandruff.
Flowers: Contain rosmarinic acid, an anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms the scalp, reducing oiliness and itchiness.
Stems: Abundant in rosmarinic acid and other antioxidants that help regulate sebum production and promote a healthy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning when dew is still present and the plant is at its peak potency.
Clean the harvested leaves by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a soft-bristled brush.
Dry the cleaned leaves in a single layer on paper towels or a clean cloth.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder and seal them tightly to preserve the herbal content.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with oily hair because they contain the soothing and purifying properties of aloe vera, which can help balance the scalp's natural oil production. The gel extracted from the aloe plant has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce oiliness and prevent the buildup of sebum, leading to healthier-looking hair. Regular consumption of aloe barbadensis capsules may help regulate the scalp's natural moisture balance, reducing the appearance of oily hair. This can result in a more manageable and cleaner-looking mane.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: It helps reduce the production of sebum, a natural oil produced by the hair follicles, thereby controlling oily hair.
Chrysophanic acid: It has astringent properties, which help to dry the scalp and reduce excess oil, making it an effective remedy for oily hair.

Phenolic acids: They help to inhibit the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to excessive oil production on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in aloe vera gel, which is known to regulate oil production and reduce dandruff.
Leaves: Aloe vera leaves also contain vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other antioxidants that help to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce oiliness.
Leaves: The astringent properties in aloe vera leaves can help to tighten the pores on the scalp, thereby reducing oil production and preventing oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh aloe barbadensis gel from mature plants with yellow tips.
Dry the gel in a clean environment at 50 degrees celsius for 48 hours.
Grind 30 grams of the dried gel into a fine powder using a blender.
Mix 15 grams of the powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 5 grams of water to form a paste.
Fill each capsule shell with 0.5 grams of the paste and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia capsules helps with oily hair because they contain the essential oil of lavender, which has natural astringent and antiseptic properties. These properties help to balance the scalp's natural oil production, reducing excess sebum and oiliness. The oil also has a calming effect on the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to oily hair. Additionally, lavender oil has been shown to improve blood circulation, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Regular use of Lavandula angustifolia capsules may lead to a reduction in oily hair.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the scalp, thereby controlling oily hair.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce the appearance of oily hair.
Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair and scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production.
Leaves: Lavender leaves are also used because of their ability to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation, making them beneficial for oily hair.
Seeds: Lavender seeds are sometimes used in hair care products due to their ability to control oil production and promote a healthy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak blooming time in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Mix 1 tablespoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 1 tablespoon of gelatin powder to create a uniform blend.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture in 250mg dosages.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus capsules helps with oily hair because they contain the natural herb Lemongrass, known for its ability to balance scalp oil production. The capsules' active ingredients can reduce dandruff and itchiness, while also controlling sebum levels. Additionally, lemongrass has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help prevent scalp infections, promoting a healthy scalp environment. This leads to a reduction in oiliness and a balanced hair texture, resulting in a fresher and cleaner appearance. Regular use can lead to improved scalp health.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps reduce oil production in the scalp by inhibiting the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase, which is responsible for converting testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to oily hair.
Limonene: Acts as an astringent, helping to control oil production and reduce the appearance of oily hair by tightening the pores and preventing sebum from accumulating.
Geraniol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which help to control the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms on the scalp that can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cymbopogon citratus leaves are commonly used in hair care products due to their high oil content and ability to help control oily hair.
Rhyzomes: Cymbopogon citratus rhyzomes are used to make capsules for oily hair because they contain lemongrass oil, which helps balance scalp oil production.
Leaves: Cymbopogon citratus leaves are also used in hair care products due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help prevent scalp infections and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems from a reputable source.
Dry the plant material further in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 2 grams of dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 50 milliliters of distilled water to form a gel.
Fill 10 empty capsules with the gel mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with oily hair because they promote a healthy scalp and improve circulation, reducing the excess oil production that leads to greasy locks. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe an itchy scalp and reduce flaking. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba capsules may help to regulate the sebaceous glands, leading to a balanced oil production and a healthier, more manageable mane. This leads to a fresher, cleaner look.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help to reduce oil production in the scalp, leading to less oily hair.
Terpenoids: They have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing the appearance of oily hair.
Bilobalide: It helps to improve blood flow to the scalp, which may help to reduce oil production and promote a healthier scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for oily hair due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help control oil production and soothe scalp irritations.

Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for oily hair due to their ability to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to excessive oil production.

Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for oily hair due to their astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance scalp pH and reduce bacterial growth that contributes to oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-60 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source to ensure quality.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a single layer at 50-60°c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 20-30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 2-3 teaspoons of the ginkgo biloba powder with 1-2 teaspoons of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill 100-200 empty vegetarian capsules with the ginkgo biloba gelatin mixture and seal tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis capsules helps with oily hair because of its unique properties. The herb, also known as soapwort, has been traditionally used to clean and purify the scalp. Its natural saponins act as a gentle cleanser, removing excess oil and dirt without stripping the hair of its natural moisture. This helps to regulate oil production, reducing the appearance of oily hair and scalp. Regular use of Saponaria officinalis capsules can promote a healthy scalp balance and shiny, manageable hair.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These natural surfactants help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce excess sebum production, which can lead to oily hair.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have astringent properties that help to reduce the appearance of oily hair by controlling sebum production and tightening the scalp's pores.
Triterpenes: These compounds have antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to prevent scalp infections, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high saponin content, which helps to balance and control oil secretion in the scalp.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and regulate the scalp.
Seeds: Employed for their ability to clean and purify the scalp, helping to reduce oiliness and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried saponaria officinalis flowers at the end of the flowering season to ensure potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 35°c for 24 hours to prevent mold growth.
Grind 20g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1g of the powdered saponaria officinalis with 5g of gelatin or vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the blend and seal them immediately to maintain freshness and potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera capsules helps with oily hair because they contain the gel's key benefits in a concentrated form. The gel's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production. The capsules also contain vitamins A, C, and E, which nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth. This combination of properties helps to control sebum production, leaving hair looking healthier and less greasy. Regular use can lead to a reduction in oiliness and an improvement in hair texture.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Helps reduce oil production in the scalp by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in the production of sebum.
Beta-sitosterol: Acts as an antisebum agent, reducing the amount of sebum produced in the scalp and helping to control oiliness.
Salicylic acid: Exfoliates the scalp, removing dead skin cells and excess oil, promoting a balanced scalp environment and reducing oiliness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel from leaves is commonly used in hair care products to help control oiliness due to its ability to balance scalp pH and reduce sebum production.
Leaves: Aloe vera juice from leaves is often used in capsules to help detoxify and purify the scalp, reducing oiliness by removing impurities and excess oil.
Leaves: Aloe vera extract from leaves is used to help regulate oil production, reduce dandruff, and soothe an oily scalp, making it a popular ingredient in hair care capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh aloe vera gel from mature plants at dawn or dusk when it's most potent.
Dry the aloe vera gel in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Weigh 40g of the dried aloe vera gel and mix it with 60g of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 200 empty capsules each with 300mg of the aloe vera gel mixture.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica capsules helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antifungal properties. The Neem oil extracted from these capsules reduces sebum production, thereby controlling oiliness in the scalp. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to eliminate fungal infections that can contribute to oily hair. As a result, the scalp's natural balance is restored, and oil production is normalized, leading to healthier and more manageable hair. This makes Azadirachta indica capsules a popular choice for those dealing with oily hair.
remedy_constituents: Azadirachtin: This triterpenoid saponin helps reduce sebum production in the scalp, thereby controlling oily hair.
Neemolide: This triterpenoid saponin has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to dry out the scalp and reduce oiliness.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have a drying effect on the scalp, reducing sebum production and helping to control oily hair.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make capsules due to their high content of azadirachtin, a compound that helps to control oil production in the scalp.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules as they contain antioxidants and other compounds that help to balance oil production and reduce dandruff.
Seeds: The oil extracted from the seeds is also used in capsules to help control oiliness, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Crush 250 grams of dried azadirachta indica leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 200 grams of excipients in a bowl such as silica and cellulose.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 1000 capsules with 0.25 grams of the mixture each.
Seal the capsules with a capsule sealer to ensure the contents remain secure and fresh.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens capsules helps with oily hair because they contain saw palmetto, a natural ingredient that regulates and balances the production of sebum in the scalp. This leads to reduced oil secretion and a decrease in the greasiness of the hair. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flakiness that can contribute to oily hair. Regular use of Serenoa repens capsules may promote a healthier scalp and a more balanced hair growth cycle.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help to regulate sebum production and reduce the oiliness of the scalp, thereby controlling oily hair.
Phenolic acids: These antioxidants help to balance the scalp's oil production and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to oily hair.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the scalp, reduce oil production, and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of fatty acids and phytosterols, which help to regulate oil production in the scalp.
Roots: The roots contain similar compounds to the leaves, making them a popular alternative for addressing oily hair and scalp conditions.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) also contain the same beneficial compounds as the leaves and roots, which help to balance oil production and reduce dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Dry 2 cups of serenoa repens roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1 cup of the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powdered root with 2 tablespoons of gelatin to create a uniform mixture.
Fill 20 empty gelatin capsules with 0.25 grams of the root mixture each using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
